
Reviewed 7 Grok social signals and 18 arXiv papers. Most arXiv entries are off-topic (bridge optimization, audio-visual LLMs, bias mitigation, etc.). Two candidates meet the bar: 'Holos' covers multi-agent orchestration at web scale with architectural grounding, and 'Improving Role Consistency in Multi-Agent Collaboration' addresses orchestration design and empirical evaluation of agent coordination. The Grok social signals mostly point to framework announcement pages with unverifiable/fabricated X post URLs — excluded per policy. AutoVerifier and GrandCode are interesting but narrower in scope.

## Suggestions

- [Holos: A Web-Scale LLM-Based Multi-Agent System for the Agentic Web](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.02334) — Presents architectural design and orchestration patterns for a web-scale multi-agent system, addressing agent coordination, tool use, and production deployment at scale.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)

- [Improving Role Consistency in Multi-Agent Collaboration via Quantitative Role Clarity](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.02770) — Proposes a quantitative framework for enforcing role clarity in multi-agent collaboration, improving orchestration reliability and agent coordination consistency with empirical evaluation.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
